plan_adherence_notes: All 10 plan steps were executed and the core implementation is correct. Steps 1-8 were fully completed: D3 modules installed (individual packages as decided), useForceGraph hook created with simulation ref + diffing + alpha restart, deriveEdges utility built with clique co-membership deduplication, ForceDirectedGraph component built with hybrid React+D3 approach preserving NodeWithDetail pattern, zoom/pan via d3-zoom, drag via d3-drag with fx/fy pinning, dynamic clique overlays via bounding box, and focus highlight + status dots + probability bars + legend. Step 9 partially done: MarketDetail.tsx updated to import ForceDirectedGraph, but old BayesNetGraph.tsx was NOT removed as the plan specified ('Remove old BayesNetGraph.tsx file'). Step 10 completed: 20 new tests (ForceDirectedGraph.test.tsx + deriveEdges.test.ts) all passing, but old BayesNetGraph.test.tsx was not removed. The leftover files are dead code that should be cleaned up.
research_quality_notes: Research was thorough and well-evidenced. All 8 questions received detailed answers with specific file:line references. The critical finding about directed edges (q4/a4 - CONDITIONAL_MARGINALS has no GET endpoint) was correctly identified and the assumption was marked WRONG with clear impact analysis. The hybrid React+D3 recommendation (q2) was well-justified by the NodeWithDetail pattern dependency on React DOM ownership. Bundle size analysis (q7) was concrete (~500KB vs ~60KB). Test strategy (q8) was practical with the mock-simulation approach. Minor gap: research could have verified d3 module peer dependency requirements more concretely.
question_completeness_notes: 8 questions covered the core design decisions well: replacement vs coexistence, React+D3 integration pattern, real-time data handling, edge data source, clique overlays, click callbacks, bundle strategy, and test approach. 7 assumptions were reasonable with appropriate confidence levels, and the low-confidence a4 (0.4) was correctly flagged as the biggest risk.
missed_questions: Accessibility: How should keyboard navigation and screen reader support work for the force-directed graph? SVG interactive elements need ARIA labels and focus management.
Performance at scale: What is the expected maximum number of nodes/edges? D3 force simulation performance degrades significantly beyond ~500 nodes. Should there be a fallback or node limit?
Server-side rendering: Does the app use SSR? D3 requires DOM access which fails in SSR environments. The useEffect approach mitigates this but it wasn't explicitly considered.
Mobile/touch support: How should zoom/pan/drag behave on touch devices? d3-zoom and d3-drag have touch support but it needs explicit testing and potentially different UX.
Old component cleanup: Should BayesNetGraph.tsx be deleted or kept as a fallback? The plan assumed deletion but execution left it in place.
implementation_gaps: BayesNetGraph.tsx not removed (dead code remains in features/graph/)
BayesNetGraph.test.tsx not removed (stale tests remain in __tests__/)
No error boundary around the graph component for graceful degradation if D3 fails
onNodeClick prop defined but not wired in MarketDetail.tsx (no navigate() call passed)
reason: The implementation is solid and follows the plan faithfully on all substantive decisions. The hybrid React+D3 architecture is well-executed: useForceGraph properly manages simulation lifecycle with ref-based diffing, deriveEdges correctly computes undirected edges from cliques with deduplication, and ForceDirectedGraph preserves the NodeWithDetail lazy-loading pattern while adding zoom/pan/drag interactivity. All 20 tests pass covering loading states, empty states, truncation, focus highlighting, status dots, probability bars, legend rendering, and edge derivation logic. The two gaps (old files not removed, onNodeClick not wired) are minor cleanup items that don't affect functionality. Research and planning phases were thorough, correctly identified the biggest risk (no directed edge API), and made pragmatic decisions throughout. Approving with note that dead code cleanup should follow.
